Enable HTTP server request metrics for Prometheus/Grafana

Enable HTTP server observations and histogram distribution so that
http_server_requests_seconds_bucket and _count metrics are exposed
on the actuator/prometheus endpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix post creation to use JSON request body instead of query params

Add @RequestBody to FeedController, send content as JSON body in load
test client, and configure Redis ObjectMapper with JavaTimeModule for
proper date serialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix neo4j container startup failure caused by read-only volume mount

Neo4j 5 chowns everything under /var/lib/neo4j/ on startup, which
fails on the :ro mounted init-graph.cypher. The file is already
mounted separately in the neo4j-init sidecar container.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement news feed system with fanout architecture

Spring Boot 3.4 / Java 21 app with Nginx load balancer, Post Service
(PostgreSQL + Redis cache), Fanout Service (RabbitMQ → workers → News Feed
Cache), Neo4j graph DB for friendships, Prometheus/Grafana monitoring,
load test client, unit tests, and Docker Compose orchestration.

commit.message:
Fix symlinked skills/roles skipped during workspace staging (#3)

## Summary
- `stage_role()` creates symlinks to role/skill directories, but Go's
`os.ReadDir` `IsDir()` returns `false` for symlinks (lstat semantics)
- Wrapper silently skipped all staged roles/skills — agent workspace
ended up empty
- Added `fs.ModeSymlink` check alongside `IsDir()` for both skills and
roles loops

## Test plan
- [x] `go build` compiles
- [x] `go test ./...` passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Implement error directive, Lua rule improvements, and tup.foreach_rule

1. Error directive: `error MESSAGE` in Tupfile now produces a parse
   error with the expanded message, matching C tup behavior.

2. Lua tup.rule improvements: Now supports multiple calling conventions:
   - tup.rule('command') — single string
   - tup.rule({'inputs'}, 'command') — table + string
   - tup.rule('command', {'outputs'}) — string + table
   - tup.rule(inputs, 'command', outputs) — strings or tables
   Rule and foreach_rule now return output tables for chaining.

3. tup.foreach_rule: New function for foreach-style rules from Lua,
   matching C tup's Lua API.

4. lua_value_to_string_vec helper: Converts Lua strings or tables to
   Vec<String> for flexible argument handling.

Test results: t0000-t2179 now 60/194 passing (up from 55).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refactor API design: replace REST anti-pattern with GraphQL persisted queries

The explore page API was stuffing unrelated data (market_health, top_gainers,
btc_header, eth_header) into a GET /v1/assets REST endpoint — a composite
page query disguised as a resource endpoint. This contradicted the design's
own "REST over GraphQL" argument.

Changes:
- Reframe explore page API as GraphQL with persisted queries (matching
  what Coinbase actually uses in production), CDN-cacheable via hash URLs
- Keep REST for simple resource endpoints (search, detail, trending)
- Fix inconsistent data types: all prices now decimal strings across all
  endpoints (list, detail, search, chart candles)
- Fix inconsistent field names: standardize on percent_change object
- Separate Redis keys per GraphQL resolver (no more duplicating
  market_health across 1,400 page blobs)
- Clarify trending REST endpoint is for non-explore-page consumers
- Update HLD diagram, pre-computation pipeline, scale tables, summary

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct EUR dashboard display for EU projects

- fmtMoney: add isEurBase guard to always show € for EU projects
- loadDisplayCurrency: default to 'eur' for EU context (not 'usd')
- dispCurToggleHTML: use 'eur' currency code for EU toggle button (not 'local')

These three fixes together resolve the dashboard showing wrong amount
(e.g. $2241 instead of €2600) for EU-based projects.

commit.message:
feat: initial AdStack MVP — full-stack newsletter ad marketplace

Two-sided marketplace connecting newsletter publishers with advertisers
using AI-powered subscriber embeddings for precision ad matching.

Stack: Fastify 5 + Drizzle ORM + PostgreSQL/pgvector + Vite + React 19 +
TanStack Query + Tailwind v4. Monorepo with pnpm workspaces + Turborepo.

Includes:
- 13-table database schema with pgvector embeddings
- JWT cookie auth with argon2 password hashing
- Publisher API: profile, newsletters CRUD, ad slots CRUD
- Buyer API: profile, campaigns CRUD, creatives CRUD
- React SPA: auth pages, role-aware dashboard, newsletter & campaign
  management, profile settings
- 6 Architecture Decision Records (ADRs)
- Docker Compose for PostgreSQL + Redis
- Shared Zod schemas between frontend and backend

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
OpenCosmos: The Homecoming (#36)

* chore: OpenCosmos founding — rename from Sage, create founding docs

The ecosystem transitions from "Sage" to "OpenCosmos" — returning to
the original Cosmo identity (March 2025) with deeper philosophical
grounding. This is Phase 0 + Phase 1a of the migration.

Founding documents:
- WELCOME.md — The front door to OpenCosmos
- packages/ai/COSMO_SYSTEM_PROMPT.md — Cosmo's voice and values
- CHRONICLE.md — Living narrative record of the founding
- OPENCOSMOS_MIGRATION.md — Full migration project plan

Directory renames:
- packages/sage-ai/ → packages/ai/
- apps/sageos/ → apps/cosmos/ (cosmOS)
- apps/sage-stocks/ → apps/stocks/

Updated all documentation (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, DESIGN-PHILOSOPHY.md,
README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CHANGELOG.md) for OpenCosmos naming.

Root package.json name: ecosystem-monorepo → opencosmos
Stocks package.json: @ecosystem/sage-stocks → @opencosmos/stocks
INCEPTION.md timestamped as historical record.

All 3 apps build successfully. No code logic changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The lockfile was out of sync after renaming apps/sage-stocks → apps/stocks.
Removed lint/typecheck/test/bundle-size steps that targeted @thesage/ui,
which lives in the separate opencosmos-ui repo and does not exist here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use 16-bit data size for IMU/Accel/Gyro sensor streaming

RVR firmware only streams IMU (0x0001), Accel (0x0002), Gyro (0x0004)
when configured with DATA_SIZE_16BIT (0x01) — not 32-bit. Locator
(0x0006) still uses 32-bit. Added normalize_uint16 and UINT16_MAX.
Each sensor gets its own token to stay within BLE ATT MTU limit.

commit.message:
feat: Add Edit Input Audio feature (trim, cut, mute, join) (#514)

## Summary

@coderabbitai ignore

Adds a complete "Edit Input Audio" feature that lets users optionally
trim, cut, mute, or join input audio before processing begins. This is
especially useful for live recordings from YouTube where users need to
skip talking/intros.

**5 phases implemented:**
- **Phase 1 (Backend):** 3 new job states, FFmpeg audio operations
service, 6 API endpoints, edit stack undo/redo
- **Phase 2 (Sessions):** Audio edit session persistence with Firestore
+ GCS, SHA256 deduplication
- **Phase 3 (Creation Flow):** Opt-in toggle in job creation,
requires_audio_edit flag across all 3 creation paths
- **Phase 4 (Editor UI):** Full audio editor with waveform
visualization, selection-based editing, playback controls, submit flow
- **Phase 5 (Restore):** Auto-save hook, session restore dialog, manual
save progress

**Key changes:**
- 3 new job states: `awaiting_audio_edit`, `in_audio_edit`,
`audio_edit_complete`
- Server-side FFmpeg operations (lossless FLAC internally, OGG Opus for
browser)
- Collapsible audio edit toggle in VisibilityStep (defaults to off)
- "Edit Audio" action button in JobCard for awaiting_audio_edit status
- Session auto-save every 3 edits + on page hide + before submit
- Session restore dialog when returning to an interrupted edit

## Test plan

- [x] All 706 frontend tests pass (38 suites)
- [x] All 2491 backend tests pass (1 pre-existing failure in unrelated
test)
- [x] 23 AudioEditor component tests (loading, rendering, undo/redo,
submit, restore, save)
- [x] 25 audio edit route tests
- [x] 18 audio edit service tests
- [x] 13 audio edit session tests
- [x] 5 VisibilityStep toggle tests
- [ ] Manual E2E test with real audio file (user to verify)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve WebSocket realtime update failures across client and server

Client sockets were never joining Socket.IO rooms after REST-based room
operations, causing all io.to(roomId) broadcasts to be silently dropped.
This was the root cause of most real-time update failures.

Fixes applied:
- Emit room:join/room:leave socket events after REST room operations
- Enrich room:state_update payload with full player data (isAI, isHost, displayName, joinedAt, aiDifficulty)
- Add monotonic version counter to prevent stale state overwrites
- Populate gameState in state:full_sync on reconnect (was always null)
- Cancel active games on grace period expiry via session manager
- Disconnect socket on logout to prevent stale connections
- Wait for game:started socket event before navigating to game page
- Fix latency measurement to use actual RTT instead of config constant
- Tighten SocketErrorPayload.code to union type on client
- Add 5s polling for lobby room list updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add principles_coverage gate to DPRD prompt and validator

Implement EEK-FINDING-1 fix from ER-CONSOLE-001: product-craftsmanship
and product-discovery-principles files are now required named inputs in
the DPRD prompt with mandatory cross-reference coverage table. Validator
adds a principles_coverage hard gate (gate 9).

commit.message:
Defer Docker re-enable until after MIG device enumeration

midclt call docker.update '{"nvidia": true}' triggers a sysext overlay
remount that temporarily removes /usr/bin/nvidia-smi from disk. This
was causing nvidia-smi -L to fail with "No such file or directory"
when enumerating MIG UUIDs.

Fix: move docker.update after MIG instances are created and enumerated.
Add a catch-all docker.update before the final summary for non-MIG and
failure paths.
